 377 + ## COMPLETED: Email Reply Drafting System (Session 48)

 378 +

 379 + ### What was built

 380 + - **Gmail MCP workflow** — Claude Code reads unread inbox, classifies (DRAFT/FLAG/SKIP), drafts replies in Steven's voice, creates threaded Gmail drafts. Steven opens inbox, tweaks, sends.

 381 + - **Response playbook** (`memory/response_playbook.md`) — 14 categories with real snippets from 150+ sent emails: pricing, budget constraints, scheduling, pilots, support routing, procurement, grants, etc.

 382 + - **Voice profile updated** (`memory/voice_profile.md`) — added Drafting Rules, Anti-AI-Tell Checklist, and 10 Learned Corrections from live testing

 383 + - **Workflow instructions** (`prompts/reply_draft.md`) — full step-by-step for any Claude Code session

 384 + - **GAS bridge `delete_draft`** — added to Code.gs for cleaning up orphaned drafts

 385 + - **Draft log** (`memory/draft_log.md`) — for future "learn from my sends" learning loop

 386 +

 387 + ### Known issues

 388 + - **Outreach browser extension** strips API-created draft body for contacts in Outreach's database. Workaround: create standalone "COPY THIS" draft for copy-paste.

 389 + - Always use `contentType="text/html"` for clickable links

 390 + - Never create duplicate drafts on same thread (causes persistent "Draft" label)

 391 +

 392 + ### Testing results

 393 + - 5 emails drafted during testing, 3 sent successfully. Voice was accurate, content corrections captured as learnings.

 394 + - Banned phrase: "Want to hop on a quick call" — replaced with "Want to connect via Zoom this week?"

 395 + - Pricing rule: push for Zoom first, send pricing after pushback or for international leads

 396 +

 ⎿   Added 49 lines

 730 - `fuzzy_match_name()` uses token subset (2+ token containment = 0.95 score) + Jaccard similarity with 0.7 threshold

 731 - Reengagement overview uses single `get_sequences()` API call (no per-prospect scanning) for fast display

 732 - `_last_reengagement_sequences` global caches overview for Mode 2 index lookup

 733 +

 734 + ---

 735 +

 736 + ## Session 48 (2026-04-07): Email Reply Drafting System

 737 +

 738 + ### What was built

 739 + - **Email Reply Drafting system** — Claude Code workflow using Gmail MCP (`gmail_search_messages`, `gmail_read_thread`, `gmail_create_draft` with `thread Id` + `content Type="text/html"`). Reads unread inbox, classifies emails (DRAFT/FL

 + AG/SKIP), drafts replies in Steven's voice, creates threaded HTML drafts directly in Gmail. Steven's workflow: open inbox → see draft → tweak → send → next.

 740 + - **Response playbook** (`memory/response_playbook.md`) — 14 categories extracted from 150+ real sent emails (100 replies + 50 outbound). Categories: pricing, budget constraints, not ready yet, info gathering, scheduling, free trial/p

 + ilot, no-pressure follow-up, brief acknowledgments, procurement/PO, support routing, unsubscribe, product info, grant funding, referral/handoff. Plus "No Reply Needed" skip list.

 741 + - **Voice profile updated** (`memory/voice_profile.md`) — 3 new sections added: Drafting Rules (pricing call-first preference, no feature fabrication, no combative tone, budget strategy, drive toward calls), Anti-AI-Tell Checklist (13

 + banned patterns), Learned Corrections (10 specific fixes from live testing).

 742 + - **Workflow instructions** (`prompts/reply_draft.md`) — complete step-by-step for any Claude Code session. Includes classification rules, energy matching, CC handling, draft log format, and "learn from my sends" workflow.

 743 + - **GAS bridge `delete_draft`** — new `delete Draft()` function in Code.gs + `case "delete_draft"` route. Uses `Gmail App.get Drafts()` to find by ID, then `.delete Draft()`. Deployed as new version. Called via Python `requests.Session().

 + post()` (curl doesn't handle GAS redirects).

 744 + - **Draft log** (`memory/draft_log.md`) — rolling 14-day log of drafted replies (thread_id, sender, subject, category, full draft text) for future learning loop comparisons.

 745 +

 746 + ### Key decisions

 747 + - **Gmail MCP over GAS bridge for drafting** — Gmail MCP supports `thread Id` for proper threading + `content Type="text/html"` for clickable links. GAS bridge `create_draft` doesn't support threading.

 748 + - **No Python modules needed** — this is a Claude Code workflow, not a Scout/Railway feature. All intelligence lives in memory files (voice profile, playbook) and prompt file (workflow instructions).

 749 + - **Drafts go directly to Gmail** — Steven explicitly requested this. No showing drafts in Claude Code for approval first. Create in Gmail immediately, show brief summary.

 750 + - **Static playbook over dynamic few-shot retrieval** — Steven's inbound categories are predictable (8-10 types cover 90%+ of emails). Static playbook is faster, more reliable, and simpler than searching Gmail for similar past replies

 + at draft time.

 751 +

 752 + ### Testing results (5 emails)

 753 + 1. Kate Grow (AI question) — DRAFT, sent successfully

 754 + 2. Anthony Pollina (locked out of levels) — FLAG, Outreach extension stripped body, used copy-paste workaround

 755 + 3. Krista Farris ("wasn't me") — DRAFT, sent successfully

 756 + 4. Kirk Ellern (budget constraint) — DRAFT, sent successfully

 757 + 5. Maree Pascall (library use case) — DRAFT, created in Gmail

 758 +

 759 + ### Learned corrections (saved to voice profile)

 760 + - Ask specific budget questions, not generic ("under a specific amount?" vs "What does your budget look like?")

 761 + - Add encouragement for budget-constrained prospects ("Don't give up yet!")

 762 + - Mention grant writing specialist when budget is the issue

 763 + - Frame CTAs with incentives ("we'll go over options AND I can set you up with trial licenses")

 764 + - Use "Let's connect via Zoom" not "Want to hop on a quick call" (BANNED — too salesy)

 765 + - Always say "Zoom" not "call" for meetings

 766 + - Keep free license offers flexible ("for a little while" not specific timeframes)

 767 + - Ask scoping questions on separate lines

 768 + - Acknowledge time gaps ("A lot has likely changed since we last connected")

 769 + - Never fabricate product features — use frames if unsure

 770 + - Default to pushing for Zoom before sending pricing (send pricing after pushback or for international)

 771 +

 772 + ### Known issues

 773 + - **Outreach browser extension conflict** — for contacts in Outreach's database, the extension takes over Gmail compose and strips API-created draft body. Workaround: create standalone draft with "COPY THIS" subject for copy-paste. Af

 + ter sending, delete orphaned threaded draft via GAS bridge.

 774 + - **Never create duplicate drafts on same thread** — causes persistent "Draft" label that requires GAS bridge deletion to fix.

 775 + - Gmail MCP has no `delete_draft` tool — must use GAS bridge for cleanup.

 776 +

 777 + ### Implementation notes

 778 + - Two-pass playbook building: Pass 1 surveyed 150 subjects/snippets (lightweight). Pass 2 read 50-70 full threads across key categories.

 779 + - Quality gate: 3+ of 5 test drafts must be usable with minor tweaks before proceeding to learning loop.

 780 + - GAS bridge `delete_draft` iterates all drafts with `Gmail App.get Drafts()` and matches by ID string. Returns `{success, deleted}` or `{success: false, error}`.

 781 + - Python `requests.Session()` handles GAS redirects properly. `urllib` and `curl` do not (302 redirect drops POST body or returns 405).

 377 + ## COMPLETED: Email Reply Drafting System (Session 48)

 378 +

 379 + ### What was built

 380 + - **Gmail MCP workflow** — Claude Code reads unread inbox, classifies (DRAFT/FLAG/SKIP), drafts replies in Steven's voice, creates threaded Gmail drafts. Steven opens inbox, tweaks, sends.

 381 + - **Response playbook** (`memory/response_playbook.md`) — 14 categories with real snippets from 150+ sent emails: pricing, budget constraints, scheduling, pilots, support routing, procurement, grants, etc.

 382 + - **Voice profile updated** (`memory/voice_profile.md`) — added Drafting Rules, Anti-AI-Tell Checklist, and 10 Learned Corrections from live testing

 383 + - **Workflow instructions** (`prompts/reply_draft.md`) — full step-by-step for any Claude Code session

 384 + - **GAS bridge `delete_draft`** — added to Code.gs for cleaning up orphaned drafts

 385 + - **Draft log** (`memory/draft_log.md`) — for future "learn from my sends" learning loop

 386 +

 387 + ### Known issues

 388 + - **Outreach browser extension** strips API-created draft body for contacts in Outreach's database. Workaround: create standalone "COPY THIS" draft for copy-paste.

 389 + - Always use `contentType="text/html"` for clickable links

 390 + - Never create duplicate drafts on same thread (causes persistent "Draft" label)

 391 +

 392 + ### Testing results

 393 + - 5 emails drafted during testing, 3 sent successfully. Voice was accurate, content corrections captured as learnings.

 394 + - Banned phrase: "Want to hop on a quick call" — replaced with "Want to connect via Zoom this week?"

 395 + - Pricing rule: push for Zoom first, send pricing after pushback or for international leads

 730 - `fuzzy_match_name()` uses token subset (2+ token containment = 0.95 score) + Jaccard similarity with 0.7 threshold

 731 - Reengagement overview uses single `get_sequences()` API call (no per-prospect scanning) for fast display

 733 +

 734 + ---

 735 +

 736 + ## Session 48 (2026-04-07): Email Reply Drafting System

 737 +

 738 + ### What was built

 739 + - **Email Reply Drafting system** — Claude Code workflow using Gmail MCP (`gmail_search_messages`, `gmail_read_thread`, `gmail_create_draft` with `thread Id` + `content Type="text/html"`). Reads unread inbox, classifies emails (DRAFT/FL

 + AG/SKIP), drafts replies in Steven's voice, creates threaded HTML drafts directly in Gmail. Steven's workflow: open inbox → see draft → tweak → send → next.

 740 + - **Response playbook** (`memory/response_playbook.md`) — 14 categories extracted from 150+ real sent emails (100 replies + 50 outbound). Categories: pricing, budget constraints, not ready yet, info gathering, scheduling, free trial/p

 + ilot, no-pressure follow-up, brief acknowledgments, procurement/PO, support routing, unsubscribe, product info, grant funding, referral/handoff. Plus "No Reply Needed" skip list.

 741 + - **Voice profile updated** (`memory/voice_profile.md`) — 3 new sections added: Drafting Rules (pricing call-first preference, no feature fabrication, no combative tone, budget strategy, drive toward calls), Anti-AI-Tell Checklist (13

 + banned patterns), Learned Corrections (10 specific fixes from live testing).

 742 + - **Workflow instructions** (`prompts/reply_draft.md`) — complete step-by-step for any Claude Code session. Includes classification rules, energy matching, CC handling, draft log format, and "learn from my sends" workflow.

 743 + - **GAS bridge `delete_draft`** — new `delete Draft()` function in Code.gs + `case "delete_draft"` route. Uses `Gmail App.get Drafts()` to find by ID, then `.delete Draft()`. Deployed as new version. Called via Python `requests.Session().

 + post()` (curl doesn't handle GAS redirects).

 744 + - **Draft log** (`memory/draft_log.md`) — rolling 14-day log of drafted replies (thread_id, sender, subject, category, full draft text) for future learning loop comparisons.

 745 +

 746 + ### Key decisions

 747 + - **Gmail MCP over GAS bridge for drafting** — Gmail MCP supports `thread Id` for proper threading + `content Type="text/html"` for clickable links. GAS bridge `create_draft` doesn't support threading.

 748 + - **No Python modules needed** — this is a Claude Code workflow, not a Scout/Railway feature. All intelligence lives in memory files (voice profile, playbook) and prompt file (workflow instructions).

 749 + - **Drafts go directly to Gmail** — Steven explicitly requested this. No showing drafts in Claude Code for approval first. Create in Gmail immediately, show brief summary.

 750 + - **Static playbook over dynamic few-shot retrieval** — Steven's inbound categories are predictable (8-10 types cover 90%+ of emails). Static playbook is faster, more reliable, and simpler than searching Gmail for similar past replies

 + at draft time.

 751 +

 752 + ### Testing results (5 emails)

 753 + 1. Kate Grow (AI question) — DRAFT, sent successfully

 754 + 2. Anthony Pollina (locked out of levels) — FLAG, Outreach extension stripped body, used copy-paste workaround

 755 + 3. Krista Farris ("wasn't me") — DRAFT, sent successfully

 756 + 4. Kirk Ellern (budget constraint) — DRAFT, sent successfully

 757 + 5. Maree Pascall (library use case) — DRAFT, created in Gmail

 758 +

 759 + ### Learned corrections (saved to voice profile)

 760 + - Ask specific budget questions, not generic ("under a specific amount?" vs "What does your budget look like?")

 761 + - Add encouragement for budget-constrained prospects ("Don't give up yet!")

 762 + - Mention grant writing specialist when budget is the issue

 763 + - Frame CTAs with incentives ("we'll go over options AND I can set you up with trial licenses")

 764 + - Use "Let's connect via Zoom" not "Want to hop on a quick call" (BANNED — too salesy)

 765 + - Always say "Zoom" not "call" for meetings

 766 + - Keep free license offers flexible ("for a little while" not specific timeframes)

 767 + - Ask scoping questions on separate lines

 768 + - Acknowledge time gaps ("A lot has likely changed since we last connected")

 769 + - Never fabricate product features — use frames if unsure

 770 + - Default to pushing for Zoom before sending pricing (send pricing after pushback or for international)

 771 +

 772 + ### Known issues

 773 + - **Outreach browser extension conflict** — for contacts in Outreach's database, the extension takes over Gmail compose and strips API-created draft body. Workaround: create standalone draft with "COPY THIS" subject for copy-paste. Af

 + ter sending, delete orphaned threaded draft via GAS bridge.

 774 + - **Never create duplicate drafts on same thread** — causes persistent "Draft" label that requires GAS bridge deletion to fix.

 775 + - Gmail MCP has no `delete_draft` tool — must use GAS bridge for cleanup.

 776 +

 777 + ### Implementation notes

 778 + - Two-pass playbook building: Pass 1 surveyed 150 subjects/snippets (lightweight). Pass 2 read 50-70 full threads across key categories.

 779 + - Quality gate: 3+ of 5 test drafts must be usable with minor tweaks before proceeding to learning loop.

 780 + - GAS bridge `delete_draft` iterates all drafts with `Gmail App.get Drafts()` and matches by ID string. Returns `{success, deleted}` or `{success: false, error}`.

 781 + - Python `requests.Session()` handles GAS redirects properly. `urllib` and `curl` do not (302 redirect drops POST body or returns 405).

 761 + - Add encouragement for budget-constrained prospects ("Don't give up yet!")

 762 + - Mention grant writing specialist when budget is the issue

 763 + - Frame CTAs with incentives ("we'll go over options AND I can set you up with trial licenses")

 764 + - Use "Let's connect via Zoom" not "Want to hop on a quick call" (BANNED — too salesy)

 765 + - Always say "Zoom" not "call" for meetings

 766 + - Keep free license offers flexible ("for a little while" not specific timeframes)

 767 + - Ask scoping questions on separate lines

 768 + - Acknowledge time gaps ("A lot has likely changed since we last connected")

 769 + - Never fabricate product features — use frames if unsure

 770 + - Default to pushing for Zoom before sending pricing (send pricing after pushback or for international)

 771 +

 772 + ### Known issues

 773 + - **Outreach browser extension conflict** — for contacts in Outreach's database, the extension takes over Gmail compose and strips API-created draft body. Workaround: create standalone draft with "COPY THIS" subject for copy-paste. Af

 + ter sending, delete orphaned threaded draft via GAS bridge.

 774 + - **Never create duplicate drafts on same thread** — causes persistent "Draft" label that requires GAS bridge deletion to fix.

 775 + - Gmail MCP has no `delete_draft` tool — must use GAS bridge for cleanup.

 776 +

 777 + ### Implementation notes

 778 + - Two-pass playbook building: Pass 1 surveyed 150 subjects/snippets (lightweight). Pass 2 read 50-70 full threads across key categories.

 779 + - Quality gate: 3+ of 5 test drafts must be usable with minor tweaks before proceeding to learning loop.

 780 + - GAS bridge `delete_draft` iterates all drafts with `Gmail App.get Drafts()` and matches by ID string. Returns `{success, deleted}` or `{success: false, error}`.

 781 + - Python `requests.Session()` handles GAS redirects properly. `urllib` and `curl` do not (302 redirect drops POST body or returns 405).

 Session 48 Summary:

 Built the Email Reply Drafting system end-to-end. Gmail MCP reads your unread inbox, classifies emails (DRAFT/FLAG/SKIP), drafts replies in your voice, and creates threaded HTML drafts directly in Gmail. You open your inbox, tweak, send, next. Built
 a 14-category response playbook from 150+ real emails, updated your voice profile with anti-AI-tell rules and 10 learned corrections from live testing, and added delete_draft to the GAS bridge for cleanup. Tested on 5 real emails — 3 sent
 successfully, discovered Outreach extension conflict (workaround documented).
